items.title: Astera Labs Expands Taurus Family with Industry’s First OCP-Standard Footprint Compatible 3.2T Smart Retimers and Smart Redrivers
items.why: Astera Labs launches industry-first OCP-standard 3.2T Ethernet Smart Retimers and Redrivers for AI scale-up/scale-out, directly relevant to high-speed interconnect in AI datacenter racks.

items.title: Intel and Fortinet Collaborate to Advance Cybersecurity Innovation and Strengthen Global Supply Chain Resilience
items.why: Intel Foundry's first named external customer on Intel 4 node is Fortinet's SP6 security ASIC, a concrete foundry business signal for Intel's external fab strategy.

items.title: TSMC eyes price hikes of up to 25% on chip production services in 2027, report claims — plans to raise baseline prices by 5% to 10% on advanced nodes
items.why: TSMC reportedly planning 5–25% wafer price increases in 2027 across advanced nodes, a major cost signal for every AI accelerator and packaging customer.

items.title: Z.AI to Use Only Chinese AI Chips at New Giant Data Center
items.why: Z.ai completes a 1 GW data center stocked exclusively with domestic Chinese chips, marking a concrete milestone in China's effort to build Nvidia-free AI infrastructure at scale.

items.title: Microsoft adopts AMD Helios AI platform for Azure expansion
items.why: Microsoft is deploying AMD's next-gen Helios AI platform across Azure alongside new EPYC VMs, confirming AMD as a second-source AI accelerator supplier to a top hyperscaler.

items.title: Vertiv Expands Global Manufacturing Capacity for AI-Ready Data Center Cooling Solutions
items.why: Vertiv is expanding manufacturing at its Italy campus to meet rising demand for advanced thermal/cooling infrastructure, a capacity signal for AI datacenter power delivery.

items.title: Siemens to acquire Defacto Technologies to complement its EDA portfolio with automated SoC design creation
items.why: Siemens acquires Defacto Technologies to add automated SoC design-creation to its EDA portfolio, consolidating its position against Synopsys/Cadence in the AI chip design tool market.

items.title: Air Products to Expand Integrated Gas Supply Network for Semiconductor Manufacturer in Taiwan
items.why: Air Products wins long-term agreement to supply process gases to multiple new Taiwan fabs and back-end packaging facilities, signaling continued advanced packaging capacity expansion.

items.title: BlackRock, Hillhouse, Temasek Said Set to Back Innolight Listing
items.why: BlackRock, Hillhouse, and Temasek as cornerstone investors in Innolight's ~$8B Hong Kong IPO signals major institutional validation of optical transceiver supply chain.

items.title: Broadband suspended lithium tantalate Mach-Zehnder modulator achieving a 460 Gbit/s net data rate
items.why: Lithium tantalate Mach-Zehnder modulator demonstrates 460 Gbit/s net data rate, a new benchmark for next-gen electro-optic platforms competing with LiNbO3.

items.title: TSMC to Hike Chip Prices by Up to 10% in 2027, Nikkei Says
items.why: TSMC discussing up to 10% wafer price hikes for 2027 directly affects cost structures for every AI accelerator and optical chip customer.

items.title: SEMI forecasts global semiconductor equipment sales to reach record $229.5bn in 2028
items.why: SEMI mid-year forecast projects record $229.5B equipment market by 2028, with 2026 at $165.9B, quantifying the capex cycle underpinning AI infrastructure build-out.

items.title: Daeduck Electronics to Invest 497 Billion Won in Semiconductor Substrates
items.why: Daeduck Electronics commits cumulative ₩710B+ to semiconductor substrate capacity expansion, signaling continued advanced packaging supply investment.

items.title: ThAME: 3D Memory-Enabled Heterogeneous Accelerator for LLM Mixture of Experts
items.why: ThAME proposes a 3D memory-enabled heterogeneous accelerator specifically targeting MoE LLM inference bottlenecks including memory bandwidth and expert routing latency.

items.title: Piping specialist Georg Fischer sees liquid cooling orders double in first half of 2026
items.why: Georg Fischer reports liquid cooling pipe orders doubling in H1 2026, a concrete demand signal for datacenter thermal infrastructure build-out pace.

items.title: [News] AMD’s Helios AI Rack Challenges NVIDIA with HBM4 Memory Edge; Microsoft Joins as Latest Customer - TrendForce
items.why: AMD's Helios AI rack with HBM4 and Microsoft as a new customer signals direct competition with NVIDIA's rack-scale platform at the HBM4 generation.

items.title: AMD challenges Nvidia as Korea’s Samsung and SK hynix win major HBM4 orders - Chosunbiz
items.why: Samsung and SK Hynix winning HBM4 orders for AMD Helios confirms multi-vendor HBM4 supply chain is activating ahead of the Nvidia-only era.

items.title: Samsung, SK Hynix prioritize DDR5 as margins near HBM levels - digitimes
items.why: Samsung and SK Hynix prioritizing DDR5 as margins approach HBM levels signals a near-term supply shift that could tighten HBM availability.

items.title: Multichamber Plasma CVD Processing Systems Market Forecast Points Higher Toward 2035 on Advanced Packaging Demand - IndexBox
items.why: SEMI forecasts semiconductor equipment sales hitting a record high in 2028 driven by AI infrastructure investment, relevant for capex and supply cycle timing.

items.title: Z.ai Builds Gigawatt Data Center on Chinese Chips Alone - Unite.AI
items.why: Z.ai completing a 1 GW data center running entirely on Chinese-made chips is a strategic infrastructure milestone that tests domestic chip viability at scale.

items.title: Google Stock: Alphabet Readies AI Chip With Built-In Gemini Brains - Investor's Business Daily
items.why: Google's 'Frozen v2' TPU reportedly optimized specifically for Gemini models, signaling continued internal silicon specialization at the hyperscaler level.

items.title: Huahsu expands capacity to tap AI and advanced packaging demand - digitimes
items.why: Huahsu expanding packaging capacity for AI and advanced packaging demand is a supply signal for CoWoS-adjacent substrate/interposer capacity.

items.title: Hanmi Semiconductor speeds up HBM tool output and plants US flag as equipment shortage looms - digitimes
items.why: Hanmi Semiconductor accelerating HBM bonding tool output and establishing US presence highlights looming equipment bottleneck for HBM production.

items.title: China's Moonshot pauses Kimi subscriptions amid hot demand, IPO push - Reuters
items.why: Tesla AI5 taping out at Samsung Foundry on a 2nm-class node is a concrete foundry design-win and advanced packaging demand signal.

items.title: Neocloud Iren signs $2.8bn in customer contracts, raises 2026 annual run rate to $4bn+
items.why: Iren securing $2.8B in contracts and raising its annual run-rate to $4B+ is a concrete demand signal for GPU cluster capacity from AI developers.

items.title: TaiSol expects server revenue to top 50% in 2H26, liquid cooling to exceed 25% for full year - digitimes
items.why: TaiSol forecasting server revenue above 50% and liquid cooling above 25% of full-year revenue in 2H26 provides a quantified thermal market signal.

items.title: NbAs Nanowires Show Lower Resistivity as Interconnect Dimensions Shrink (Cornell, NYCU, IBM et al.)
items.why: Cornell/IBM paper on NbAs Weyl semimetal nanowires showing lower resistivity at scaled dimensions is a potential long-term successor to copper interconnects.

items.title: Microsoft to Deploy Next-Gen AMD Instinct and AMD EPYC Processors as the Companies Expand Their Long-Term Strategic Partnership
items.why: Primary source: Microsoft commits to next-gen AMD Instinct GPUs and EPYC CPUs across Azure, including the Helios Rackscale solution—one of AMD's largest hyperscale AI infrastructure wins.

items.title: Z.AI Completes Giant Data Center With Chinese Chips to Train AI - Bloomberg.com
items.why: Z.AI reportedly completed a 1-gigawatt AI data center in China trained entirely on domestic chips, a significant milestone for Chinese AI self-sufficiency.

items.title: IEEE Study Highlights How Micro-Transfer Printing Can Lead to Advanced Silicon Photonics
items.why: IEEE study shows micro-transfer printing enables heterogeneous material integration in silicon photonics, a key technique for scaling CPO and advanced SiPh transceiver manufacturing.

items.title: Intel To Add Support for Gen 2 MRDIMMs and Faster DDR5 RDIMMs to Xeon 6 Platform
items.why: Intel is adding DDR5-8000 RDIMM and Gen 2 MRDIMM support to Xeon 6 mid-generation, pushing server memory bandwidth on existing platforms.

items.title: Samsung, SK hynix push new memory layer as AI outgrows HBM alone - The Korea Herald
items.why: Samsung and SK Hynix are developing a new memory tier beyond HBM to address AI workloads that have outgrown HBM bandwidth and capacity limits.

items.title: Samsung could overtake SK Hynix in the HBM market by 2027 - digitimes
items.why: Digitimes reports Samsung could overtake SK Hynix in HBM market share by 2027, a major potential shift in the HBM supply landscape.

items.title: Will in-house CXL development undermine their own DRAM business? Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron simultaneously abandon in-house CXL controller development. - Moomoo
items.why: Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron have reportedly all abandoned in-house CXL controller development simultaneously, raising questions about CXL's near-term roadmap.

items.title: NVIDIA NVLink: The Scale-Up Network for AI Factories | NVIDIA Technical Blog - NVIDIA Developer
items.why: NVIDIA technical blog details NVLink architecture as the scale-up fabric for AI factories, providing specs and topology relevant to rack-scale AI infrastructure design.

items.title: TYLsemi Raises $43 Million to Advance Chiplet-Based AI Infrastructure - Electronics For You BUSINESS
items.why: TYLsemi raised $43M to advance chiplet-based AI infrastructure, signaling continued investor appetite for advanced packaging startups.

items.title: ASML to offer employees €20,000 retention bonus for staying in 2027-2030 - Reuters
items.why: ASML is offering €20,000 retention bonuses to keep employees through 2030, indicating concern about talent retention as High-NA EUV ramps.

items.title: ASML Considers Price Increase for Low-NA EUV, TSMC Unhappy - TechPowerUp
items.why: ASML is reportedly considering a price increase for low-NA EUV systems, with TSMC reportedly unhappy—relevant to foundry cost structures and packaging economics.

items.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Building Life Science Industry’s Most Advanced AI Factory on NVIDIA Vera Rubin
items.why: Bristol Myers Squibb is deploying a second NVIDIA Vera Rubin AI factory cluster, marking one of the first named Vera Rubin enterprise deployments outside hyperscalers.

items.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
items.why: Google is reportedly developing a new custom chip specifically to run Gemini inference more efficiently, potentially reducing reliance on TPUs or GPUs for inference workloads.

items.title: IREN Signs $2.8bn in New Customer Contracts with Leading AI Developers, Raises 2026 ARR Target to over $4bn
items.why: IREN signed $2.8B in new multi-year AI cloud contracts and raised its 2026 ARR target to over $4B, with 85% of target now under contract—a strong neocloud demand signal.

items.title: Hut 8 signs 352MW lease with unnamed hyperscaler in Texas
items.why: Hut 8 signed a 352MW lease with an unnamed hyperscaler at its Texas Beacon Point campus, fully leasing the site and confirming continued hyperscaler capacity absorption.

items.title: ABB to acquire silicon carbide power conversion firm - Engineering.com
items.why: ABB is acquiring a silicon carbide power conversion firm, extending SiC M&A activity into industrial/datacenter power infrastructure.

items.title: Inference Accelerator that Integrates Compute-in-Interconnect and Memory to Mitigate the Memory Wall (NUS)
items.why: NUS researchers published CIMERA, a compute-in-interconnect and memory accelerator for LLM inference that addresses the memory wall with reconfigurable precision.

items.title: Microsoft will deploy AMD’s Helios rack-scale AI accelerator ‘at scale’ on Azure – Radeon Instinct MI455X and Epyc Venice power will be available through Redmond’s cloud infrastructure
items.why: Microsoft committing to deploy AMD's Helios rack-scale AI system (MI455X + EPYC Venice) 'at scale' on Azure is a direct competitive signal against NVIDIA in hyperscale AI infrastructure.

items.title: Memory chip boss admits RAM prices are 'abnormally high' — SK Group chairman considering building a semiconductor plant in the US to expand supply, calm ‘chipflation’
items.why: SK Group chairman publicly acknowledging 'abnormally high' RAM prices and weighing a US fab signals both supply-side pressure and geopolitical reshoring dynamics relevant to HBM/DRAM markets.

items.title: Machine Learning-Driven Design of Mixed-Pitch Grating Couplers for Co-Packaged Optics Applications
items.why: ML-optimized mixed-pitch grating coupler design targeting CPO applications directly addresses a key coupling-efficiency bottleneck in silicon photonics interconnects.

items.title: Who Needs DRAM? We Have Fiber
items.why: Proposes using optical fiber memory as a DRAM alternative for AI workloads, a novel architectural concept relevant to the memory-bandwidth bottleneck in AI infrastructure.
